transcript.clean_text: one of the most visited cities in the whole of the tour de france's long history poor hosted the final mountain stage of this 108th race short in terms of distance just under 130 kilometers but packing a real punch in terms of climbing over three and a half thousand meters to be climbed most of it in the final third the mighty tomale perhaps the most famous mountain in the history of cycling before a finish for the ninth time at luzada plenty of hairpins to the top of the ski station some tough gradients as well the last test in the mountains before three days approaching the finale in paris unsure of what to expect at the start mate maharich went out on the attack he'd be joined by two other riders before julian ala philippe and pierre perry also tried to get across the gap was never really allowed to grow though behind mark cavendish had a very good start of the day yet more points to extend his lead in the green jersey competition and as they approached the halfway stage he was still in the main bunch that all changed on the first slopes of the tour malay however and counter attacks from the main bunch began david gaudu was piloted up to the front by vanot madwes the king of the mountains would be played out as well woods and pools trying their best to pick up points over the top but it would be lato and gordu who'd go over first 35 kilometers to go from the top of the tour malay and the first big decision in the gc was made approaching it dropped and dropping out of the top five overall david gordo would open the wonderful gap on pia lator would be caught by those chasing almost a minute opened up on the descent but when we got to the final climb gordo would just be hanging out in front before eventually being reeled in by the ineos grenadiers looking for their first bit of success of this tour pools hanging on at the back he'd be hoping that today bulgachar wouldn't take his jersey off him but pogaccha wanted to win again two-stage victory so far rafael mica took up the pace in the final few kilometers fanart and company dropped to what's micah was done for gachar again made his move once more the top three in the race were the strongest on the climbs vinegar following also there was kara paz he was in third place in the gc cepcus did his best to try and set things up for viengagot and embrace mass was having one of his better days mass tried to take advantage and continue the long run of spanish victories at lusada five of the previous eight finishes had been won by his compatriots pogata was having none of that however he set off in search past the man from majorca and in the final few hundred meters nobody would get even into the slipstream of the slovenian karapas and vingor giving their best but road behind pogacha and the only open road in front was between him and the finish line third stage went at this race second in a row and pogaccha with an unassailable lead seemingly in the general classification second of ingot third to kara paz that's how it lies as well in the general classification mass on the stage finishing in fourth and moving up in the gc2 it will be another day where they came in behind in ones and twos then o'connor moving up in the gc after a good date but today pogaccha winning by two seconds bonus seconds going to winger gordon karapaz as well and winger got moving further ahead of karapaz still though with a narrow lead in the gc in second place not a good day for regula he lost nine minutes to the winner a winner on the day and again in the yellow jersey tate dominating the world of pro cycling dominating this tour 5 45 now ahead of wingago who's six seconds ahead of kara pass o'connor the fourth keldaman into fifth and dropping from fourth to tenth the mountains are done but the tour isn't finished quite yet stage 19 looks to be won for either a breakaway or even a sprint finish it's a long stage too 207 kilometers directly north into the region around bordeaux and finishing in libon the gc looks finished but there's always drama around the corner at this tour de france three days to go to paris what's gonna happen next you
